articles.title: OPPORTUNITY
articles.summary: Just last month, an iconic Picasso painting shattered expectations when it sold for $139 million at auction. Impressive, considering it was purchased for _around $1 million _in the late 1960’s. But there’s a surprising group of investors also celebrating this sale: 61,000 everyday users of one investment platform [5].

articles.title: META’S AUDIO FOUNDATION MODEL
articles.summary: Meta recently teased a foundation AI model for audio. It has released the paper along with more samples and compelling demos. The main goal of the project was to generate controllable audio content with styles all from the same model.

articles.title: MICROSOFT AND LABOR UNIONS FORM ‘HISTORIC’ ALLIANCE ON AI
articles.summary: Microsoft is partnering with the American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations to discuss AI's impact on workers and to provide AI training from winter 2024. This groundbreaking alliance aims to involve workers in AI development and policy shaping and includes a neutrality agreement to ease unionization at Microsoft.

articles.title: EU REACHES DEAL ON THE WORLD’S FIRST COMPREHENSIVE AI RULES
articles.summary: The European Union has reached a tentative agreement on the world's first comprehensive artificial intelligence legislation, known as the Artificial Intelligence Act. The deal, achieved after lengthy negotiations, addresses various aspects of AI, including generative AI and law enforcement's use of facial recognition, and sets a legal framework for AI usage, with potential global implications for AI regulation and development.

articles.title: BIOCLIP
articles.summary: A vision model designed for biology applications. It outperforms OpenAI's clip by almost 20% on specific bio tasks. A 10m paired images and text training set is also available.

articles.title: VIDEO CLASSIFICATION WITH SPATIAL-TEMPORAL DIVERSITY
articles.summary: Spatial-Temporal Diversification Network (STDN) is a new model that captures a variety of cues in videos by exploring both spatial features within frames and relationships across time.

articles.title: ENHANCING RL ALGORITHMS WITH STATE-ADAPTIVE BALANCING
articles.summary: Researchers introduce FamO2O, a framework designed to boost the capabilities of current offline-to-online RL algorithms by determining the best balance for improvement and constraints based on the state.

articles.title: BRICKSLLM
articles.summary: BricksLLM is a cloud-native AI gateway written in Go that serves as a proxy to OpenAI and lets you create API keys that have rate limits, cost limits, and TTLs.

articles.title: AI AGENTS WITH LANGUAGE MODEL CAPABILITIES
articles.summary: KwaiAgents is an advanced agent system that uses large language models to mimic human-like cognitive skills.

articles.title: NOW ADD A WALRUS: PROMPT ENGINEERING IN DALL-E 3
PYTORCH 2 INTERNALS
articles.summary: A great recent talk about all the new things in Pytorch 2 like Dynamo, Instructor, and ExecuTorch.

articles.title: CHALLENGES IN UNSUPERVISED OBJECT SEGMENTATION
articles.summary: This project delves into the difficulty of segmenting objects in real-world images using unsupervised models.

articles.title: HUGGINGFACE GETS AMD SUPPORT
articles.summary: Transformers 4.36.0 has been released with the new Mistral model, AMD support, safetensors as default, and more!

articles.title: TOMORROW: BUILD AN AI AGENT USING REINFORCEMENT LEARNING, WITH SENIOR RESEARCH SCIENTIST RANDY COGILL
articles.summary: Interview Kickstart is offering this FREE hands-on session! Learn about reinforcement learning, the Q-learning algorithm, and Gymnasium

articles.title: AI TAMAGO
articles.summary: A local LLM pet with a web front end.

articles.title: 30X FASTER AI TRAINING
articles.summary: This project has received significant attention recently for rewriting gradients for common models by hand in Triton. Its benchmarks aren't the most compelling, but it is a great endeavor.
